source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane, Other Lifting

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Oil Leak from Tubing Connection
actions_taken: Annulus casing valve closed upstream of leak
Platform cleaned using hoses, absorbent pads, Dawn soap, and degreaser
event.summary: On 16 July 2019 at 0700 hours, approximately 4.8 barrels of oil leaked from a 3/4" broken tubing connection (Swagelok fitting) on the gas lift bleed ring of the casing valve at Well R-22 on the unmanned GI 22-R platform. The leak was discovered when an operator on nearby GI 22-L observed a sheen north of the platform. The platform had been shut-in since 28 June 2019 for maintenance, but pressure remained on the gas lift line and well casing. High seas from Hurricane Barry (14-16 ft with occasional 21 ft seas) caused well movement that sheared the tubing. Contributing factors included inadequate tubing support, improperly functioning check valves in gas lift mandrels, incorrect installation of a Flow Safety Valve upstream rather than downstream of the tubing connection, and cleanup procedures that exacerbated pollution to a wildlife refuge.
recommendations: BSEE NOD recommends that OSM and OII develop a safety alert to address tubing supports. Tubing supports should be installed in such a way as to prevent stress and sheering.

hazards.name: Gas lift system with check valves

threats.name: Tubing connection shearing due to well movement
Inadequate tubing support under dynamic loading
Flow Safety Valve installed in incorrect location

consequences.name: Oil discharge to Gulf of Mexico
Environmental pollution and wildlife refuge impact

controls.name: Tubing support design and installation
controls.barrier_role: Prevent well movement from causing tubing shear
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to prevent tubing failure

supporting_text: Cox failed to support the tubing in such a way as to prevent well movement from sheering the fitting from the tubing
BSEE NOD recommends that OSM and OII develop a safety alert to address tubing supports. Tubing supports should be installed in such a way as to prevent stress and sheering.

controls.name: Check valves in gas lift mandrels
controls.barrier_role: Prevent backflow from production tubing to annulus
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to prevent pressure communication

supporting_text: Cox failed to maintain the gas lift mandrel check valves so as to prevent backflow from the production tubing to the annulus
The check valves in the gas lift mandrels failed to function as designed
The diagnostics indicated communication between the short string and the production casing through the gas lift valves causing unexpected pressure on the production casing after shut-in

controls.name: Flow Safety Valve (FSV) installation location
controls.barrier_role: Minimize pollution by stopping flow downstream of tubing connection
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to limit discharge volume

supporting_text: The platform Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs) indicated a Flow Safety Valve (FSV) downstream of the 3/4" tubing connection. However, the FSV was installed upstream of 3/4" tubing connection
Pollution may have been minimized if the FSV was installed downstream of the tubing connection
Cox failed to install the tubing connection upstream of the final gas lift FSV per approved SFD's

controls.name: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs) accuracy
controls.barrier_role: Ensure correct equipment installation per design
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent design-installation mismatch

supporting_text: Management Systems - P&IDs in error: The platform Piping and Instrumentation Diagram (P&IDs) indicated a Flow Safety Valve (FSV) flowline downstream of the 3/4" tubing connection. However, the FSV was installed upstream of 3/4" tubing connection.

controls.name: Sheen detection and reporting
controls.barrier_role: Detect and report pollution event for response
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control for early detection and notification

supporting_text: On 16 July 2019 at 0610 hours, an Operator on GI 22-L platform observed a sheen north of the platform emanating from a nearby, unmanned Cox Operating GI 22-R facility
Cox reported the sheen observation to the National Response Center (NRC), Report #1252101

controls.name: Platform cleanup procedures
controls.barrier_role: Minimize environmental impact from spilled oil
controls.lod_basis: Tertiary control for pollution containment and cleanup

supporting_text: The Operators then began the process of cleaning the oil from the platform by using hoses, absorbent pads, Dawn soap, and degreaser
Cox's method of oil clean up may have exacerbated the impact to the state wildlife refuge
Human Performance error - Not following proper procedures: The Operators washed down the platform in such a way that resulted in further pollution and impact to the state wildlife refuge

Operators were actively discharging oil into the Gulf of Mexico due to cleanup operations. Operators were applying rig soap, Dawn soap, and saltwater to remove oil residue from the facility equipment.

controls.name: Casing pressure monitoring and diagnostics
controls.barrier_role: Detect abnormal pressure conditions indicating valve failure
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to detect check valve failure

supporting_text: The Operators recorded the casing pressure from a gauge on the platform. The gauge displayed a pressure of 1201 psi for the GI R-22 well

The BSEE District Operations Support Section requested Cox to perform a Casing Pressure Diagnostic, which failed. The diagnostics indicated communication between the short string and the production casing through the gas lift valves

controls.name: Well hatch cover and barricading
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unauthorized access and ensure safe work environment

supporting_text: From the heliport, the BSEE Inspectors observed an improperly barricaded open hole
The well hatch cover for Well R-22 was removed and improperly barricaded

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
